trends_result.evidence: Interest: 19/100 now vs 2/100 six weeks ago · Peak: 100/100 · geo: IN · 90 days

social_result.evidence: Web-indexed social mentions · 5 results · Note: search results about social content, not direct Instagram data

news_result.evidence: 2 articles in past 2 months · 1 fashion-relevant signals
top_headlines: Tripti Dimri’s Punit Balana sharara set features mirror and marodi work
Eid Ul Adha 2026: Choose the Trendy Sharara Suit Style To Shine With Your Look

bet_override: Override: demand signals are weak despite active editorial/social buzz. Social buzz may not convert to value-fashion sales. Watch 4 weeks before any commitment.

india_fit.price_band_reason: Sharara sets with basic embroidery or prints are achievable at ₹699–₹1,299 in value fashion without sacrificing margin.
india_fit.climate_fit_reason: Sharara silhouettes suit festive/occasional wear in cooler months and air-conditioned spaces, but heavy fabric versions are unsuitable for summer heat; layering with kurta mitigates but adds cost.
india_fit.occasion_fit: festive, Eid, wedding, occasion wear, mehendi
india_fit.cultural_fit_reason: Sharara sets align strongly with North Indian and Muslim bridal/festive traditions and modesty norms, with established acceptance across urban and semi-urban India.
india_fit.value_fashion_fit_reason: Meesho and value-fashion customers buy occasion wear, but sharara demand is typically seasonal (Eid, weddings) and skews slightly upmarket; impulse repeat purchase risk is lower than everyday kurtis.

claude_synthesis.convergence_summary: Sharara sets show real recent interest spike (Google Trends 19→100) driven by festive/occasion demand and celebrity styling, but marketplace presence is thin (10 listings) and search volume remains below baseline for core value-fashion categories.
claude_synthesis.signal_agreement: Demand and buzz partially align: Google Trends and news coverage confirm interest, but Myntra/Meesho listings and discount intensity suggest cautious seller adoption and unproven conversion at value price points.
claude_synthesis.disagreement_note: Direct conversion metric is missing—track: (1) Meesho sharara set sell-through rate vs. regular kurta SKUs over 8 weeks, (2) average discount depth and frequency, (3) Tier 2–3 city search volume vs. Tier 1 to confirm whether demand is concentrated or distributed.
claude_synthesis.bet_reasoning: Google Trends spike to 19/100 and peak of 100/100 in recent weeks, plus Tripti Dimri celebrity signal and Eid news mentions, indicate genuine seasonal occasion demand; however, only 10 marketplace listings and 3 discount signals suggest sellers are testing, not scaling. Recommend a **cautious pilot**: stock 15–20% of kurta volume in 2–3 high-demand SKUs (mirror work/marodi, festive colors) at ₹899–₹1,199 for Eid (Feb–March) and wedding season (Dec–Jan), with clear exit plan if sell-through underperforms regular kurtis by >30%.
claude_synthesis.skepticism_flag: Google Trends interest spike may reflect search volume from wedding/Eid research (high intent, low frequency) rather than repeat purchase intent; celebrity mentions (Tripti Dimli, Punit Balana) skew toward premium positioning, risking misalignment with value-fashion buyer psychology and margin compression if sourcing costs for embroidery/work exceed projections.
